preamble: The palatalized voiceless alveolar fricative /sʲ/ can undergo a phonetic transformation to the voiced postalveolar fricative /ʒ/. This shift typically involves both a change in the place of articulation from alveolar to postalveolar, often as a result of continued palatalization, and a voicing process, frequently occurring in intervocalic positions or through assimilation.

examples.note: The Latin intervocalic /s/ before /io/ (which would induce palatalization to /sʲ/) developed into /ʒ/ in Walloon.
